pages.raw_text:
H. M. Stephens, attorney in the
famous rate case, when interro-
gated this morning as to the FEAST
bility and practicability of the rail
roads accepting a terminal rate
franchise, said:


Il reiterate that which I have
many times said. that there is no
legal or practical reason why the
railroads cannot accept franchises
with terminal rate provisions.


ANY decision by the interstate
commerce commission will require
readjustment of rates at least
from st. Paul west. The rates from
ST. Paul west are the ones which
receive excessive rates that are
paid by this city and the Inland
Empire.


Il wish that you would inform
the public that my course in this
matter has been determined and is
followed by reason of the facts and
the law as | have learned them in
connection with the freight rate
case. l feel so thoroughly convinced
that terminal rates to Spokane and
the Inland Empire would be equit-
able and just that they ought to be
secured by any means that are
legal or legitimate"
